title: rosa canina subsp. cariotii
intro: Rosa canina subsp. cariotii, also known as the wild rose or dog rose, is a subspecies of rose native to the Iberian Peninsula and Mediterranean regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Rosa canina subsp. cariotii is believed to have various health benefits, such as improving cardiovascular health, reducing inflammation, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies like teas, infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various forms.
